description:
GOAL: Heap-allocated bitstrings preserving fz's existing match/construct semantics (see src/bitstr.rs and Pattern::Bitstring / Expr::Bitstring in src/ast.rs).

## Decisions captured

- Bitstring layout: HeapHeader (16) + bit_len:u64 (8) + bytes (variable, padded to 8 bytes). Total = 24 + ceil(bit_len/8) padded.
- One allocation per bitstring; supports zero-copy slicing later via "view" objects (refcount-equivalent at object level via GC reachability — no separate refcount needed, GC handles dependent buffer lifetime naturally).
- Bitstring matching reuses existing matcher logic (src/bitstr.rs); lowered to bit-extraction primitives in IR.
- HeapKind::Bitstring tracer: raw payload, nothing to recurse into.

## Deliverables

- IR prims for bit extraction (read_int / read_float / read_binary / read_bits) and construction (bitstring_from_fields).
- Codegen for `<< ... >>` builders (Expr::Bitstring -> alloc + bit-pack).
- Codegen for bitstring patterns (Pattern::Bitstring -> bit-extract + bind).
- HeapKind::Bitstring branch in .11.2 tracer (no inner FzValues to trace; it's raw bytes).

## Tests

- `~b[0xff, 0xab]` allocates and prints.
- `<<n::8, rest::binary>> = <<...>>` matches.
- Existing bitstring fixtures pass (build_packet / parse_packet from main.rs SAMPLE constant).
- Variable-size fields (`<<len::8, data::binary-size(len)>>`).
- GC traces bitstrings (allocates, drops, frees correctly).

## Open

- Zero-copy slicing — defer the "view" type to a follow-up. v1 always copies on slice if needed.
title: Bitstrings as heap type
